title: Police / Authority Impersonation
description: Scammers impersonate police or CBI, claiming there is a warrant for the victim's arrest or illicit items were found in a parcel.
exampleTranscript: This is the police. We have a warrant for your arrest due to illegal items found under your name. Pay the fine immediately or you will be arrested.
recommendedAction: Do not share personal details. Hang up and contact your local authorities directly.

title: Bank Account Suspension
description: Scammers pose as bank or RBI officials, claiming the victim's account is suspended and demanding details to reactivate it.
exampleTranscript: This is a call from RBI. Your bank account has been suspended due to suspicious activity. Please provide your card details and OTP to verify.
recommendedAction: Never share OTPs or card details. Call your bank using the number on the back of your card.

title: KYC Update Fraud
description: Scammers claim the victim's KYC is incomplete or expired and threaten to block their services if they don't update it immediately.
exampleTranscript: Dear customer, your KYC is pending. Your account will be blocked in 24 hours. Click the link or provide OTP to update KYC immediately.
recommendedAction: Do not click unverified links. Update KYC only through official bank apps or branches.

title: Customs / Courier Scam
description: Scammers claim a parcel containing drugs, passports, or illegal items has been intercepted in the victim's name by customs.
exampleTranscript: FedEx customer service here. A parcel in your name was seized by customs because it contains illegal passports. We are transferring you to the cyber police.
recommendedAction: Disconnect immediately. Do not pay any clearance fees. Legitimate couriers do not transfer calls to police.

title: UPI Collect Request Fraud
description: Scammers deceive victims into approving a UPI collect request by claiming they are sending money, but actually withdrawing it.
exampleTranscript: I am sending you the money on UPI. Just scan the QR code and enter your PIN to receive the payment.
recommendedAction: Entering a UPI PIN is only for sending money, never for receiving. Decline the request.

title: High-Yield Investment Fraud
description: Scammers promise unrealistically high returns on investments, often involving cryptocurrency or stock tips.
exampleTranscript: Join our VIP trading group. We guarantee 50% returns in one week on crypto investments. Just deposit to this address.
recommendedAction: Avoid unsolicited investment offers. If it sounds too good to be true, it is a scam.

title: Tech Support Scam
description: Scammers claim the victim's computer or phone is infected with a virus and demand payment for support services.
exampleTranscript: Microsoft technical support. Your computer is infected with a critical virus. Download this software so we can fix it for $200.
recommendedAction: Do not give remote access to your device. Legitimate tech companies do not call you proactively about viruses.

title: Lottery / Prize Scam
description: Victims are told they won a large sum of money or a car but must pay a processing fee or tax upfront to claim it.
exampleTranscript: Congratulations, you have won the grand prize of 50 lakh rupees! To claim it, you just need to pay a small processing tax of 5000 rupees.
recommendedAction: You never have to pay upfront to receive a legitimate prize. Ignore the claim.

title: Job Offer Fraud
description: Scammers offer lucrative work-from-home jobs (like liking YouTube videos) but require an initial deposit or 'training fee'.
exampleTranscript: We are hiring for a part-time job. Earn 5000 daily by rating hotels. Just pay a 1000 rupee registration fee to start.
recommendedAction: Legitimate employers never ask you to pay for a job. Do not send money.

title: Family Emergency Scam
description: Scammers impersonate a loved one claiming to be in an accident or jail, begging for immediate funds.
exampleTranscript: Dad, I am in the hospital after an accident. My phone is broken. Please send money quickly to this doctor's number.
recommendedAction: Hang up and verify the story by calling your family member's known phone number.
